url_parameter.description: Open redirect payloads for URL-based redirect parameters (redirect, url, next, return, goto)

payloads.description: Absolute URL to external domain
Protocol-relative redirect bypass
Escaped slash bypass — some parsers unescape before redirect
Quadruple slash bypass — parser normalisation edge case
Missing slashes — lenient parsers accept scheme:host without //
At-sign URL authority confusion
Subdomain of attacker domain ending with target
URL-encoded double slash after domain
Null byte injection to truncate domain check
Target domain as query param to bypass domain checks
Unicode full-width dot (U+3002) to bypass dot-based domain filters
Backslash after path — some parsers treat backslash as forward slash

protocol.description: Redirect payloads using protocol-level tricks (javascript:, data:)

payloads.description: JavaScript protocol redirect — yields XSS
JavaScript protocol with trailing comment to bypass path checks
data: URI redirect — browser executes contents as HTML
Base64-encoded data: URI to bypass content filters
Mixed case JavaScript protocol bypass
CRLF in protocol name to bypass string matching
CRLF header injection — inject Location header directly
VBScript protocol redirect (IE/Edge legacy)

path_based.description: Open redirect via path manipulation without explicit URL parameter

payloads.description: Absolute URL as path — some routers follow it
URL-encoded slash to form protocol-relative URL
Domain as path — relies on open proxy or path-based redirect
Dot-prefixed domain in path
Tab character before slash to bypass path validation
Fragment injection to mask real destination
Triple slash — some parsers strip leading slashes
Double encoded path separator confusion

encoding_bypass.description: Encoding and obfuscation tricks to bypass redirect URL validation

payloads.description: Fully URL-encoded https://evil.com
Double URL-encoded slashes
Unicode ideographic full stop (U+3002) as dot replacement
Unicode lookalike characters in domain
URL-encoded dot-dot after domain
URL-encoded scheme separator
Backslash before @ to confuse authority parsing
Hex IP address (example: 66.102.7.6)
